末.origin: Take a tree 木 and add a long line 一 right at the very top — that mark points to the tip, the end.

sentence.en: On the weekend we go to the park.

列.origin: The knife 刂 on the right slices things into neat pieces, lining them up in a row — that's a line.

sentence.en: We line up in a row.

购.origin: The shell 贝 was old money, so this character means to spend money — to buy something.

sentence.en: Mom goes to the store to shop.

单.origin: Two little dots 丷 sit alone on top — picture one single thing standing by itself.

sentence.en: This problem is very simple.

约.origin: A silk thread 纟 ties two friends together with a promise — that's making an appointment.

sentence.en: We agreed to meet tomorrow.

品.origin: Three mouths 口 stacked up — when everyone is talking about something, it must be a fine product.

sentence.en: This gift is really pretty.

挑.origin: A hand 扌 reaching out to pick the best one — that's how you choose and pick things.

sentence.en: I picked an apple.

折.origin: A hand 扌 swinging an axe 斤 to chop wood — snap, the branch breaks!

sentence.en: We fold paper together.

裙.origin: The clothes radical 衤 on the left tells you it's something you wear — a pretty skirt.

sentence.en: Little sister is wearing a red skirt.

错.origin: The metal radical 钅 plus 昔 'long ago' — like gold once held but now lost, it means a mistake.

sentence.en: I wrote one character wrong.

颜.origin: The head 页 on the right shows this is about your face — its look and its colors.

sentence.en: What color do you like?

露.origin: Rain 雨 sitting on top of a road 路 — tiny drops of rain left on the grass are dew.

sentence.en: In the morning there is dew on the grass.

滩.origin: Water 氵 that is hard 难 to cross — a rushing, rocky beach where the river runs fast.

sentence.en: We play on the sandy beach.

搭.origin: A hand 扌 piecing things together — stacking and joining blocks to build something up.

sentence.en: Little brother likes building with blocks.

油.origin: The water radical 氵 shows a liquid, and 由 gives the sound — slippery, shiny oil.

sentence.en: There is a lot of oil in the pan.

换.origin: A hand 扌 swapping one thing for another — trade this, take that, you change them.

sentence.en: Let's switch seats.

聊.origin: The ear 耳 on the left listens — when ears are busy, friends are happily chatting.

sentence.en: I chat with my friend.

烤.origin: Fire 火 on the left does the work — hold food over the flames to roast and bake it.

sentence.en: We go to have a barbecue together.

躺.origin: The body 身 on the left tells you what you do — stretch your body out and lie down.

sentence.en: He lies on the bed and reads.

浪.origin: Water 氵 plus 良 for the sound — the sea pushes up rolling, splashing waves.

sentence.en: The waves come one after another.

者.origin: Think of it as a little tag pointing at a person — 'the one who' does something.

sentence.en: He is a reporter.

始.origin: The woman radical 女 on the left, with 台 for the sound — a mother begins new life, so it means to start.

sentence.en: Class has begun.

民.origin: An old shape standing for ordinary working people — together they are the people, the citizens.

sentence.en: I love my people.

族.origin: People gathered under one flag 方, swearing together — a family, a clan, a whole group.

sentence.en: China has many ethnic groups.

舞.origin: Picture two feet 舛 stepping below while hands wave fans above — that's dancing!

sentence.en: Big sister loves to dance.

捏.origin: A hand 扌 squeezing and shaping — pinch and press the clay to mold a little shape.

sentence.en: We mold a little bird out of clay.

勇.origin: The strength radical 力 at the bottom gives the power you need to be brave.

sentence.en: He is a brave child.

敢.origin: A hand holding a stick 攵 striking at something fierce — you need courage to dare to do that.

sentence.en: I dare to sleep by myself.

艺.origin: The grass radical 艹 on top — long ago planting things skillfully was an art.

sentence.en: Drawing is a kind of art.

彩.origin: The three soft strokes 彡 on the right are like brush hairs sweeping bright colors.

sentence.en: There are colorful clouds in the sky.

巧.origin: The work radical 工 on the left — doing work neatly and cleverly makes you skillful.

sentence.en: Grandma has a pair of skillful hands.

脏.origin: The flesh radical ⺼ on the left tells you it's part of the body — your inside organs.

sentence.en: The heart is inside our body.

害.origin: Under a roof 宀 something sharp hides — be careful, it can hurt and harm.

sentence.en: I am not afraid of the dark.

惊.origin: The heart radical 忄 on the left jumps — your heart leaps when something gives you a fright.

sentence.en: This is a big surprise.

轻.origin: The cart radical 车 on the left — an empty cart is easy to pull, nice and light.

sentence.en: This box is very light.

算.origin: Bamboo ⺮ on top sits over a counting tool — bamboo sticks once helped people calculate.

sentence.en: The teacher teaches us arithmetic.

坏.origin: The earth radical 土 plus 不 'not' — soil that is no good has gone bad and rotten.

sentence.en: There is a bad person in the story.

反.origin: A hand 又 pressed up against a cliff 厂 — pushing the other way means the opposite.

sentence.en: Their opinions are opposite.

狐.origin: The dog radical 犭 on the left tells you it's an animal — a clever, bushy-tailed fox.

sentence.en: There is a fox in the forest.

葡.origin: The grass radical 艹 on top shows it grows from a plant — sweet, juicy grapes on a vine.

sentence.en: I like eating grapes.
